case.paper_title: Caste as an Impediment to Trade
case.focal_claim: The study finds substantially higher income for low-caste households residing in villages dominated by BACs compared to villages dominated by upper caste.

original_datasets.name: household
original_datasets.columns: [hhcode, village, caste, borrow, bihar, literate, totinc, cropincacre, totland, landirr, paddy, wheat, cereal, pulse, bulb, seed, cash, canal, river, noalkal, nolog, nosoil, noflood, bus3, tele3, ps3, pds3, bank3, pps3, ms3, ss3, phc3, hosp3, electric, hhelec, area, pmixdominant, tolaparea, gwdevelopment, rainfall, gwavailability, domlow, dist1, dist2, dist3, dist4, dist5, dist6, dist8, dist9, dist10, dist11, dist12, dist13, dist14, dist16, dist17, dist18, dist19, dist20, dist21, dist22, dist23, dist24, dist25]

summary_statistics.info: DataFrame with 1666 rows and 85 columns. Mixed types: numeric and categorical (e.g., caste, borrow, electric, ownpump, buywater). Key variables: totinc (float), domlow (0/1 with 371 missing), bihar, literate, totland, landirr, multiple distance and infrastructure controls.

original_datasets.notes: domlow is missing for 371 observations and is dropped before modeling. Outcome is heavily right-skewed; analysis uses log transformation of totinc.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without additional restriction.

analysis_path.path_description: Compute log(totinc), perform one-sided t-test comparing BAC-dominated vs. upper-caste dominated villages, fit multiple OLS models with robust SEs predicting log(totinc) from domlow and controls, and perform Blinder-Oaxaca twofold decomposition.

control_variables: bihar, literate, borrow, totland, landirr, dist1, dist2, dist3, dist4, dist5, dist6, dist8, dist9, dist10, dist11, dist12, dist13, dist14, dist16, dist17, dist18, dist19, dist20, dist21, dist22, dist23, dist24, dist25, area, pmixdominant, tolaparea, gwdevelopment, rainfall, gwavailability, river, canal, noalkal, nolog, nosoil, noflood, paddy, wheat, cereal, pulse, bulb, seed, cash, bus3, tele3, ps3, pds3, bank3, pps3, ms3, ss3, phc3, hosp3, electric, hhelec, caste
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Listwise deletion by model after excluding missing domlow.
key_choices.data_processing: Create log_totinc and convert categorical variables via formula coding (C()).
key_choices.model_choice: OLS with robust (HC0) standard errors; plus one-sided Welch t-test on log_totinc; Blinder-Oaxaca twofold decomposition with Reimers weights (0.5).
key_choices.inference_rule: p-value threshold 0.05 for domlow coefficient (robust SEs); one-sided t-test p-value for mean difference.

outcome.name: log_totinc
outcome.definition: Natural log of total household income (totinc).

main_predictor.name: domlow
main_predictor.definition: Indicator for village dominated by lower castes (BACs): 1=low-caste dominated, 0=upper-caste dominated.

analysis_code.notes: Python translation of original R analysis; reads /app/data/AEJApp-2009-0289-data/household.dta. Produces t-test, robust OLS domlow coefficient summaries, and Oaxaca twofold decomposition.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow the planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

tasks.task_instruction: Do not use District controls, Crop controls, Distance controls, Groundwater controls, Public goods controls. Do not include Irrigation, credit, and tenancy variables and their interaction terms. Do not use instruments for water buyer status.

analysis_path.path_description: Compute log(totinc) and fit OLS with robust SEs predicting log(totinc) from domlow and allowed controls only.

control_variables: bihar, literate, totland, cash, electric, caste
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Listwise deletion by model after excluding missing domlow.
key_choices.data_processing: Create log_totinc and convert categorical variables via formula coding (C()).
key_choices.inference_rule: p-value threshold 0.05 for domlow coefficient (robust SEs).

outcome.name: log_totinc
outcome.definition: Natural log of total household income (totinc).

main_predictor.name: domlow
main_predictor.definition: Indicator for village dominated by lower castes (BACs): 1=low-caste dominated, 0=upper-caste dominated.

analysis_code.notes: Python translation of the Task 2 restricted R analysis; reads /app/data/AEJApp-2009-0289-data/household.dta.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use only the allowed controls and exclusions specified; do not introduce instruments or excluded controls.

files.data/T89FM_multi100__py.py: Executes Task1: one-sided t-test on log_totinc, multiple OLS models with robust HC0 SEs including domlow and full controls, and Blinder-Oaxaca twofold decomposition.
files.data/T89FM_multi100_2__py.py: Executes Task2: restricted OLS with robust HC0 SEs including domlow and only allowed controls.

codebase.notes: Original R scripts (T89FM_multi100.R, T89FM_multi100_2.R) were translated to Python to comply with execution policy. All file IO targets /app/data. Ensure statsmodels, scipy, pandas, and patsy are installed.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original household.dta dataset and the provided Python scripts. Collect and store outputs: one-sided t-test results, domlow coefficient estimates with robust SEs and p-values, and Oaxaca twofold decomposition (Task1).
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Task1: one-sided t-test for mean log_totinc difference, domlow coefficient with robust SE and p-value across models, Oaxaca twofold explained/unexplained components; Task2: domlow coefficient with robust SE and p-value.
